join_order.notes: Build dense adsh filter and retain only EQ triples whose adsh passes SIC predicate.

join_order.build: pre(filtered: stmt='EQ' AND adsh in filtered sub)
join_order.probe: num rows from num_adsh_fk_hash postings for filtered adsh
join_order.notes: Use compact triple multiplicity map on (adsh,tag,version); within each adsh, accumulate USD non-null num rows by (tag,version) before probing pre multiplicity.
Resolve tlabel with sequential scan over tag(tag,version,abstract,tlabel) to avoid random rowid access from serialized PK map.

data_structures.aggregation: two_phase_hash_group_by: local compact_hash_map((sic,tag,version,stmt)->{sum,count}) + local compact_hash_set((sic,tag,version,stmt,cik) inserted once per adsh-group), then final hash merge to (sic,tlabel,stmt)

optimization_notes: Root cause is a combined physical/work mismatch: the current hot path still does per-row hash work in main_scan and resolves tag labels through a serialized PK map that causes random rowid accesses and cache-miss-heavy probes. Strategy Category B/C changes the plan to consolidate qualifying num rows per adsh into a compact (tag,version) map before pre multiplicity probes, reducing repeated hash lookups and DISTINCT insert attempts, then resolves tlabel by scanning tag columns sequentially for only needed keys to replace random-access PK-map probing. This keeps selective sub+pre pruning while shifting the remaining heavy work to sequential, cache-friendlier passes proportional to surviving groups.
